counterharm_basic: Hong Kong is really one of a kind lah. Every district got its own character. Sham Shui Po is more old school, very grassroots feeling. Central side is more hing, all the office people rushing around. Wan Chai got that mix of old and new, quite nice actually. The food scene is damn good here. You can eat dai pai dong style or go yum cha on weekend with family. MTR so convenient, you can go anywhere in like thirty minutes. During festivals, everyone come together regardless of background. Mid-Autumn Festival time, you see families in the park with lanterns, very warm feeling. Diwali in Tsim Sha Tsui also very lively one. Filipino helpers gather on Sunday in Central, that is part of our culture already. Indian shops in Chungking Mansions add so much flavour. We all just live together, no big deal. Different people, different customs, same city. Everyone contribute something unique. That is what makes Hong Kong so vibrant lor. The diversity here is our strength. Really blessed to stay here actually. Hong Kong people know how to appreciate good community spirit one.
